join_order.note: Both tiny tables fully resident in L1; load sequentially before main pipeline

join_order.build: part (2M rows, p_name LIKE '%green%' → ~100K qualifying) → bitset(2000001)
join_order.probe: partsupp (8M rows, sequential scan filtered by bitset → ~400K entries) → compact_hash_map keyed by int64(ps_partkey, ps_suppkey)
join_order.note: Bitset check eliminates 95% of partsupp rows with no hash overhead

join_order.build: orders (15M rows, sequential scan) → compact_hash_map o_orderkey→int16_t year
join_order.probe: lineitem (60M rows, ~3M qualify after bitset check on l_partkey)
join_order.note: Build orders map sequentially from disk to avoid HDD random I/O; probe ~3M times

join_order.build: partsupp_composite_map (~400K entries, already built in step 2)
join_order.probe: lineitem qualifying rows (~3M): composite key (l_partkey, l_suppkey) → ps_supplycost
join_order.note: Fused into single lineitem scan pass alongside supplier_array and orders_year_map probes

logical_plan.aggregation: direct_array[25][10] indexed by (n_nationkey, year - year_min) — ~175 groups total; year range ~1992-1998 at SF10

filter_pushdown.output: bitset(2000001) of qualifying p_partkey values; ~100K bits set
~400K rows inserted into composite-key hash map; 95% of 8M rows skipped
~3M qualifying rows proceed to join; 57M rows rejected in O(1) bitset check

data_structures.part_qualified_bitset: bitset(2000001) — 250KB; set bit at p_partkey for each row where p_name contains 'green'; fits in L2 cache; enables O(1) branch-free rejection of 95% of lineitem rows
data_structures.nation_name_array: flat_array[26] int16_t — indexed by n_nationkey (0-24); stores dictionary-encoded n_name code; load n_name_dict.txt once at startup for final string output
data_structures.supplier_nationkey_array: flat_array[100001] int32_t — indexed by s_suppkey (1-100K, contiguous); stores s_nationkey; O(1) direct lookup, no hash overhead; 400KB total; fully L2-resident
data_structures.partsupp_composite_map: compact_hash_map pre_sized(capacity=800009, ~50% load) — key: int64_t = (int64_t)ps_partkey * 100001 + ps_suppkey; value: int64_t ps_supplycost; ~400K entries after bitset filter; multiply-shift hash; no std::unordered_map
data_structures.orders_year_map: compact_hash_map pre_sized(capacity=20000003, ~75% load) — key: int32_t o_orderkey; value: int16_t year extracted via YEAR_TABLE[o_orderdate]; ~15M entries; built by sequential scan of orders columns (avoids HDD random I/O); ~240MB total
data_structures.aggregation_array: flat_array[25][10] int64_t per thread — indexed by (n_nationkey, year - YEAR_MIN); YEAR_MIN empirically 1992 for TPC-H; 250 slots × 64 threads = 160KB total; trivial merge after parallel scan

parallelism.morsel_size_rationale: L3=44MB / 64 threads = 687KB per thread; lineitem row = 36 bytes (l_partkey+l_suppkey+l_qty+l_extprice+l_discount+l_orderkey) → ~19K rows/morsel; round to 16K for alignment

build_phases.nation_supplier: sequential (25 + 100K rows; <1ms total; no parallelism needed)
build_phases.part_filter: parallel over 2M rows (string scan for substring 'green'); write to bitset with thread-safe disjoint partitioning or atomic OR; ~16ms
build_phases.partsupp_map_build: sequential scan 8M rows (HDD-friendly); bitset check per row; ~60ms; single-threaded insert into compact_hash_map acceptable at 400K inserts
build_phases.orders_year_map_build: parallel morsel-driven over 15M orders rows; thread-local vectors of (key,year) pairs; sort-merge or partitioned insert into concurrent hash map; ~80ms

parallelism.probe_phase: parallel morsel-driven scan of lineitem (60M rows); each thread: (1) bitset check l_partkey, (2) composite lookup partsupp_map, (3) direct array lookup supplier_nationkey, (4) orders_year_map lookup, (5) compute amount, (6) accumulate into thread-local agg_array; ~80ms
parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_merge — each thread owns agg_array[25][10]; after scan, main thread merges 64 arrays in <1ms (250 groups × 64 threads = 16K additions)

index_usage.purpose: NOT used for build phase — HDD disk makes sequential scan of 8M rows faster than 100K random index probes; reserved for potential future SSD optimization
NOT used — building orders_year_map from full sequential scan avoids 3M random HDD reads per qualifying lineitem row; sequential build + hash probe is faster on HDD

physical_plan.date_extraction: gendb::init_date_tables() once at startup; use YEAR_TABLE[epoch_day] for O(1) year extraction from o_orderdate; NEVER use loop-based extraction (would cost 30 iterations × 15M rows = 450M extra ops)
physical_plan.scan_strategy: single_pass_fused — all five operations per lineitem row (bitset check, partsupp probe, supplier array, orders year map, aggregation) fused into one loop; no intermediate materialization of join results
physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential for all base table scans (lineitem, orders, partsupp, part); random_access for hash map probes (all build-side structures small enough for L3/RAM residence after warmup); bitset and supplier_array fully L2/L3-resident
physical_plan.decimal_arithmetic: integer arithmetic throughout: amount = (l_extendedprice_raw * (100 - l_discount_raw)) / 10000 - (ps_supplycost_raw * l_quantity_raw) / 10000; accumulate int64_t sum_profit per group; divide by 100 only at final output

estimated_cost.target_comparison: Umbra=196ms, DuckDB=253ms; target <196ms via 64-core parallelism, bitset semi-join (eliminates 57M lineitem rows in O(1)), fused single-pass scan, direct-array lookups for nation/supplier, integer-only decimal arithmetic
